facts.text: Bethpage’s name comes from Quaker Thomas Powell, who chose it after the Biblical town Bethphage between Jericho and Jerusalem.
The Long Island Rail Road first stopped at what is now Bethpage in 1851, calling the station Jerusalem before it became Bethpage.
From 1936 to 1994, Grumman Aircraft Engineering in Bethpage built the F‑14 Tomcat, the Apollo Lunar Excursion Module, and other iconic aircraft.
The Greece Public Library opened a 42,000‑square‑foot main building in 2000 and added an award‑winning Children’s Story Garden in 2021.
The Town of Greece was originally called Northampton and split from the Town of Gates when the New York State Legislature created it in 1822.
